name: Agent4Edu Shows Bounded Model-Version and Agent-Workflow Sensitivity
statement: Agent4Edu reports a bounded exploratory GPT-4 condition with higher ACC than GPT-3.5-turbo under a 100-learner setup, while the broader method depends on an agent workflow that conditions generation on learner profile, memory, knowledge proficiency, reflection, and action prompts.
population_or_context: High-school online learning response simulation and personalized learning algorithm augmentation.
quantitative_note: arXiv HTML Table 1 reports Agent4Edu (GPT-3.5-turbo) at 73.88 ACC, Agent4Edu (GPT-3.5-turbo)100 at 74.57 ACC, and Agent4Edu (GPT-4)100 at 82.43 ACC.
caveats: The GPT-4 comparison is not a full-scale aligned model benchmark because it is marked as a 100-learner exploratory condition.
The result should be read as model-version sensitivity plus workflow evidence, not a general GPT-4-over-GPT-3.5 ranking for all synthetic response tasks.
evaluation_method: Learner response prediction metrics and downstream CAT augmentation checks.
reported_metrics: ACC values from Table 1 and CAT F1-score improvements from Table 4.
limitations: The model-version comparison is bounded and exploratory.
The setting is personalized-learning response simulation rather than essay or constructed-response scoring.
Model choice, agent workflow, and sample condition are not fully separable in the staged evidence.

source_locator.section: arXiv HTML Table 1, Section 4.2, Appendix A.3, and Appendix C.1
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The staged finding paraphrases the reported Agent4Edu GPT-3.5/GPT-4 ACC rows and the profile/memory/action workflow.
